message:
fix(native_events): promote protocol-anomaly logs to warn (ft-ltpog)

The decode-error log at `handle_connection_with_cx`'s wire-event arm
was already promoted to `warn!` (fdd1e817). Extend that fix to the
other two debug! call sites where protocol-level anomalies were
landing invisibly in production:

- connection-handler `Err` propagation: split by error kind.
  `Interrupted` is cx-driven cancellation (expected noise, stays at
  debug!); every other kind is a real post-accept I/O fault and is
  now logged at warn! with {error, error_kind, path} structured
  fields. Clean client disconnects already exit via `Ok(None)` from
  `next_line_with_cx` and never reach this arm.
- steady-state JoinError in the accept loop: split on
  `err.is_cancelled()`. Cancellation is expected; panics propagating
  out of child connection tasks now warn! with {error, path}.

Shutdown-phase JoinErrors and Drop-time socket cleanup errors stay
at debug! — documented inline as intentional.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
